key_insight: The paper improves the mixing-time guarantee of Dikin walks on polytopes from the previous $d^{2.5}$ bound to $d^{2.25}$ by using a scaled Lee--Sidford metric and a higher-order geometric analysis.
engineering_relevance: This advances affine-invariant sampling for high-dimensional constrained optimization and can improve the efficiency of randomized methods used in convex optimization, uncertainty quantification, and probabilistic inference over polytopes.
method: Uses a scaled Lee--Sidford metric to define the Dikin proposal.
Proves improved average self-concordance to raise Metropolis acceptance probability.
Combines higher-order expansion, moving orthonormal frames, and Wiener-chaos tools to control Gaussian polynomial terms.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a formal theoretical result: warm-start mixing for exponential sampling over a polytope in $d^{2.25}$ iterations, with an improved cold-start bound via annealing.
watch_item: The guarantee depends on a warm start and sophisticated barrier-geometry calculations, so practical performance may be sensitive to metric construction and implementation overhead.
paper_title: Beyond the $d^{2.5}$-mixing bound for Dikin walks on polytopes
